description: Canonical model and workflow profile metadata for MerchBench routing reports. Cost and latency indices are coarse artifact-level priors until production telemetry is available.

cluster_descriptions.Rules and heuristics: Best for deterministic gates: OTB, MOQ, margin floors, capacity, policy, and schema checks.
cluster_descriptions.Local models: Useful for no-quota experimentation and privacy-sensitive smoke tests, but current local scores are below deployable judgment thresholds.
cluster_descriptions.Small paid models: Useful for high-volume low-risk calls when deterministic validation and escalation are present.
cluster_descriptions.Free hosted open models: Useful quota pool for open-model comparators and inexpensive hosted experiments.
cluster_descriptions.Free hosted frontier models: Strong free-capacity comparators; current best free hosted run is Z.ai GLM 4.7 on Cerebras.
cluster_descriptions.Efficient paid frontier models: Best current economic frontier for broad retail judgment in the stored panel.
cluster_descriptions.Premium paid frontier models: Quality anchors for high-downside exceptions where incremental quality justifies cost and latency.
cluster_descriptions.Human reviewed workflows: Required for irreversible, high-value, safety, legal, brand, or executive exception decisions.

baseline/source_echo.notes: Adversarial scorer sanity check; anti-echo hardening keeps it below deployable thresholds.

ollama/gemma4:e2b.notes: Best local score, but many rows returned empty or parse-invalid content.

ollama/llama3.2:3b.notes: Reliable local output producer, but materially weaker judgment.

ollama/lfm2.5-thinking:1.2b.notes: Tiny local comparator with frequent response-contract failures.

openai/gpt-4.1-nano.notes: Clean older direct-OpenAI baseline, now surpassed by GPT-5 Nano.

openai/gpt-5-nano.notes: Very low-cost paid baseline with useful improvement over GPT-4.1 Nano.

groq/llama-3.3-70b-versatile.notes: Large hosted open comparator, but below the current paid frontier.

groq/meta-llama/llama-4-scout-17b-16e-in.notes: Efficient hosted baseline with moderate full-suite quality.

groq/qwen/qwen3-32b.notes: Best Groq-hosted result in the current full-suite run.

cerebras/gpt-oss-120b.notes: Strong open model and useful free-capacity comparator.

cerebras/zai-glm-4.7.notes: Strongest free hosted result in the current full-suite panel.

openai/gpt-5-mini.notes: Best cheap paid specialist for summarization, extraction, and portfolio triage.

openai/gpt-5.4-mini.notes: Current economic frontier result for broad judgment-heavy retail workflows.

openai/gpt-5.4.notes: Near-top overall quality and strongest ambiguous-judgment profile.

openai/gpt-5.5.notes: Overall quality leader; best reserved for high-downside exceptions.
